title: PeopleSoft Gains CRM Market Momentum
author: Karen E. Smith
abstract: Aberdeen Group profiles PeopleSoft's CRM market ambitions following the completion of its $1.75B acquisition of J.D. Edwards, which made PeopleSoft the second-largest enterprise software company with $2.9B in annual revenue and 11,900 customers across 25 industries. The study examines whether the newly enlarged PeopleSoft arrived too late — or just in time — to capture a leadership position in an increasingly competitive CRM market dominated by Siebel, SAP, and Oracle. Aberdeen assesses PeopleSoft's carefully orchestrated strategy to secure global enterprise software leadership.
importance_rationale: Published one week before the new fiscal year opened, this study captures PeopleSoft at its peak market position—before Oracle's hostile takeover concluded—while simultaneously assessing whether it could challenge Siebel and SAP in CRM. The analysis is historically significant as a pre-acquisition baseline of a company about to be absorbed.
relevance_rationale: CRM market consolidation dynamics, late-mover strategy in maturing software categories, and mid-market ERP expansion patterns documented here remain analytically relevant. The PeopleSoft-JDE integration playbook informs modern cloud ERP M&A strategies.
prescience_rationale: Aberdeen's optimistic framing of PeopleSoft's CRM momentum did not anticipate the Oracle acquisition completing 15 months later. PeopleSoft never achieved CRM market leadership; Oracle eventually acquired Siebel as well (2005 for $5.85B), collapsing the competitive dynamic the study analyzed.
